mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, measured by the Consumer Price Index, forces the central bank to maintain a restrictive policy stance. This increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings, leading to lower equity valuations and negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS rate is 5.33, indicating a restrictive stance.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 exhibits positive 60-day returns in over 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met during a tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) is a direct indicator of broad economic strength. This strength translates into higher aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, supporting positive equity market returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS value of 158,286 is near historical highs, suggesting a robust labor market.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS shows a negative rank correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) signals a severe slowdown in a key, interest-rate sensitive sector of the economy. This weakness often precedes broader economic downturns, leading to negative revisions in corporate earnings and equity prices.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 20% from its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a leading indicator of economic cycle turning points.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient remains below zero for a period exceeding two years.

mechanism_narrative: The rate of change in industrial production (INDPRO) serves as a proxy for the health of the manufacturing and supply side of the economy. Positive momentum in INDPRO indicates rising output, which supports corporate revenues and leads to positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The 6-month momentum of INDPRO is not positively correlated (Rank IC < 0.02) with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) concurrent with restrictive monetary policy (high FEDFUNDS) signals that policy tightening is beginning to damage the labor market. This combination is a classic recessionary indicator, leading to sharply negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is already at a high level, the market becomes highly sensitive to signals of continued hawkishness. In this regime, strong labor market data (PAYEMS beats expectations) is interpreted as 'bad news' because it implies the Fed will keep rates higher for longer, negatively impacting equity valuations over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% is well into restrictive territory.
failure_condition: The correlation between PAYEMS surprises and 5-day S&P 500 returns is positive when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) momentum is a direct measure of consumer health. A negative rate of change, especially when inflation is elevated, indicates that consumer purchasing power is eroding, which directly pressures corporate revenues and leads to negative stock returns.
failure_condition: The 3-month momentum of inflation-adjusted RSAFS has a positive rank correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger-than-expected real GDP growth (GDPC1) when the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is already low (<4.0%) signals an overheating economy. This forces the market to price in a more aggressive central bank response, leading to higher discount rates and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.0%, the correlation between real GDP growth surprises and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns is positive.
